title: Redux
objectives: Explain the single-store, action, reducer model
Understand pure reducers and unidirectional flow
Know when Redux's structure pays off

sections.markdown: Redux is a predictable state container with a single immutable store. The UI dispatches actions; pure reducers compute the next state from (currentState, action); the UI rebuilds from the new state.

Redux's three principles: a single source of truth (one store), state is read-only (change only by dispatching actions), and changes are made by pure reducers. This makes state changes predictable, traceable and time-travel-debuggable.
Putting side effects or mutations in reducers — reducers must be pure (no API calls, no mutation). Side effects belong in middleware (e.g., thunk/epics).
Redux brings the most ceremony. It shines for very large apps needing strict, auditable, time-travelable state. For most Flutter apps, BLoC/Riverpod give similar predictability with less boilerplate.

flashcards.back: Single source of truth, read-only state, changes via pure reducers.
Pure — (state, action) → new state, no side effects or mutation.

quiz.explain: Read-only state changes through dispatched actions + reducers.
Reducers are pure functions; side effects go to middleware.

quiz.q: Async/side effects in Redux live in…
quiz.explain: Middleware handles async and side effects.

challenges.text: Counter with a store, action and reducer.
Add async data loading via middleware (thunk).
Combine reducers for a multi-feature state tree.
Implement time-travel debugging by replaying actions.

interviewQuestions: What are the three Redux principles?
When is Redux worth its boilerplate?
